name: Third Wave Coffee
address: 100 Feet Road, HAL 2nd Stage, Indiranagar, Bengaluru 560038
vibe: Modern minimalist spot on the 100 Feet Road strip. Perfect for laptop work with fast Wi-Fi, great cold brews, and a focused crowd.
mustTry: Cold Brew, Pour Over, Signature Latte

name: Third Wave Coffee
vibe: Same Third Wave polish, but for the Koramangala crowd. Great spot to catch up over filter coffee or a cortado.
mustTry: Filter Coffee, Cortado, Banana Bread

name: Third Wave Coffee
vibe: Great pit-stop in the heart of the city. Busy on weekends, but the coffee is consistently good — a reliable central BLR option.
mustTry: Cold Brew, Oat Latte, Croissant
mapsQuery: Third Wave Coffee Church Street Bangalore

name: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters
vibe: One of India's best specialty roasters. The beans sourced from Indian estates shine — whether you go for a black pour-over or a creamy latte.
mustTry: Single Origin Pour Over, Cold Brew Tonic, Cappuccino
mapsQuery: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters Koramangala Bangalore

name: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters
vibe: Flagship-level ambiance in a quiet north Bengaluru neighbourhood. A calmer Blue Tokai experience away from the traffic — great for serious coffee conversation.
mustTry: Single Origin Espresso, Aeropress, Seasonal Blends
mapsQuery: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters Sadashivanagar Bangalore

name: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters
vibe: South Bengaluru's go-to Blue Tokai outpost. Calm mornings, excellent coffee, and a neighbourhood vibe that feels more like a local secret.
mustTry: Cappuccino, Cold Brew, Cinnamon Roll
mapsQuery: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters Jayanagar Bangalore

name: Matteo Coffea
address: Church Street, Ashok Nagar, MG Road, Bengaluru 560001
vibe: Tucked into the Church Street buzz, Matteo is warm and dessert-forward. The affogatos and mochas are made with care — ideal for a relaxed evening date.
mustTry: Affogato, Mocha, Tiramisu Latte
mapsQuery: Matteo Coffea Church Street MG Road Bangalore

name: Corridor Coffee
vibe: Small, serious, and stripped-back. No frills — just very good coffee in a calm corridor-style space that regulars guard jealously.
mustTry: Flat White, Aeropress, Walnut Brownie

name: Araku Coffee
address: 716, 12th Main Road, HAL 2nd Stage, Indiranagar, Bengaluru 560008
vibe: Araku valley coffee, grown by tribal farmers in Andhra Pradesh and celebrated in Paris. This is India's most internationally acclaimed coffee — and the Indiranagar cafe is beautifully designed.
mustTry: Araku Black Coffee, Honey Process Pour Over, Araku Latte

name: The Flying Squirrel
address: 877, 12th Main Rd, HAL 2nd Stage, Indiranagar, Bengaluru 560038
vibe: Coorg estate coffee roasted in-house. The space is relaxed and leafy — great weekend brunch spot with a heavy coffee pedigree. Spot the signature squirrel motifs.
mustTry: Estate Espresso, Cold Brew, Coorg Filter Coffee
mapsQuery: The Flying Squirrel Coffee Indiranagar Bangalore

name: Subko Coffee
vibe: Mumbai's cult roaster has landed in BLR with the same restrained elegance. Clean lines, thoughtful sourcing, and a very deliberate cup. Not for those in a rush.
mustTry: Single Origin Espresso, AeroPress, Signature Blend Latte

name: The Kind Roastery & Brew Room
address: 877, 12th Main Rd, HAL 2nd Stage, Indiranagar, Bengaluru 560038
vibe: A thoughtfully designed brew room in the heart of Indiranagar. Each coffee tells a story — the baristas know their stuff and the space invites lingering.
mustTry: Rotating Single Origin, Vanilla Latte, Batch Brew
mapsQuery: The Kind Roastery Brew Room Indiranagar Bangalore

name: Nerlu Cafe
address: 30, Crescent Rd, Madhava Nagar, Gandhi Nagar, Bengaluru 560001
vibe: A quiet gem on Crescent Road that stocks and serves multiple Indian specialty roasters. Perfect for discovering new beans — ask the staff for a recommendation.
mustTry: Guest Roaster Pour Over, Iced Latte, Homemade Scone

name: Chelvies Coffee
vibe: A multi-floor coffee experience on 100 Feet Road. Each floor has a distinct mood — choose your vibe, from social ground floor to quiet top-floor work nook.
mustTry: Signature Iced Latte, Cascara Tea, Banana Loaf

name: Beanlore Coffee Roasters
address: 450, 17th Cross Rd, Sector 4, HSR Layout, Bengaluru 560102
vibe: HSR's specialty anchor. Roasting on-premise with beans from Chikmagalur, Coorg and Araku. The in-house roasts are a coffee nerd's delight — pick up a bag on the way out.
mustTry: Estate Espresso, Cold Brew, Roastery Blend Pour Over
mapsQuery: Beanlore Coffee Roasters HSR Layout Bangalore

name: Beanlore Coffee Roasters
address: 503, Chinmaya Mission Hospital Rd, Indiranagar, Bengaluru 560038
vibe: The Indiranagar sibling — slightly quieter and more neighbourhood-feel than the HSR outpost. Great for a slow morning coffee ritual.
mustTry: Latte, Filter Coffee, Espresso Tonic

name: Paper & Pie
address: 842/A, 100 Feet Road, Near Metro Pillar 55, Indiranagar, Bengaluru 560038
vibe: Part cafe, part bakery, part workspace. The pies and bakes steal the show, but the coffee is proper too. A consistent favourite for weekend brunches.
mustTry: Quiche, Blueberry Tart, Filter Coffee, Iced Latte

name: The Caffeine Baar
vibe: Coffee cocktails, seasonal menus, and creative fermented brews. The Caffeine Baar pushes what a cafe can be — equal parts coffee bar and cocktail lounge vibes.
mustTry: Espresso Tonic, Cascara Collins, Signature Seasonal Brew
mapsQuery: The Caffeine Baar Church Street Bangalore

name: The Caffeine Baar
address: 1424/81, South End A Cross, Jayanagar 9th Block, Bengaluru 560069
vibe: The South BLR outpost of the experimental Caffeine Baar. Same creative drinks, slightly calmer vibe — perfect for those who want the craft without the Church Street buzz.
mustTry: Honey Cascara, Fermented Cold Brew, Miso Latte

name: Alchemy Coffee Roasters
vibe: One of South BLR's best-kept specialty secrets. Alchemy roasts small-batch Indian estate coffees with real precision — the bar work is excellent and the space is unhurried.
mustTry: Single Origin Espresso, Drip Filter, Iced Americano

name: Block Two Coffee
vibe: A tiny neighbourhood specialty cafe in Jayanagar 2nd Block that feels like a well-kept local secret. The outdoor seating is lovely and the filter coffee is spot on.
mustTry: Filter Coffee, Flat White, Seasonal Special

name: Ela Matcha
address: 575, 9th A Main Road, Stage 1, Indiranagar, Bengaluru 560038
vibe: Indiranagar's dedicated matcha bar with a South Indian twist — they do a cardamom matcha latte that's become a local cult drink. Perfect for the matcha crowd.
mustTry: Cardamom Matcha Latte, Ceremonial Grade Matcha, Matcha Cold Brew

name: Turtle Matcha
vibe: A new entrant in the Indiranagar matcha scene. Turtle Matcha focuses on ceremonial-grade Japanese matcha, served in minimal, well-designed cups. Very easy on the eyes too.
mustTry: Ceremonial Matcha, Matcha Tiramisu, Hojicha Latte

name: DYU Art Cafe
address: 23, KHB MIG Colony, 1st Cross Road, Koramangala 8th Block, Bengaluru 560095
vibe: A beloved Bengaluru institution in a converted Kerala-style bungalow. Surrounded by books, art, and cats — the kind of place you stumble into for coffee and stay for hours.
mustTry: Filter Coffee, Honey Latte, DYU Brownie

name: The Hole in the Wall Cafe
address: No 4, 8th Main Road, Koramangala 4th Block, Bengaluru 560034
vibe: Beloved for its all-day brunch menu. The waffles and eggs benedict draw queues on weekends, but the coffee stands on its own too. A timeless Koramangala classic.
mustTry: Eggs Benedict, Belgian Waffles, Cappuccino
mapsQuery: Hole in the Wall Cafe Koramangala Bangalore

name: Hatti Kaapi
address: Multiple outlets across Bengaluru — Koramangala, Indiranagar, MG Road
vibe: Bengaluru's beloved filter coffee chain that keeps the dabaara tradition alive. No frills, no fuss — just the best value cup of kaapi in the city. Always busy, always good.
mustTry: Filter Kaapi (Dabaara), Degree Coffee, Bun Butter Jam

name: MTR — Mavalli Tiffin Room
vibe: A Bengaluru institution since 1924. The filter coffee here is one of the city's most iconic experiences — pure decoction, frothy and perfectly sweet. Arrive early; queues form fast.
mustTry: Filter Coffee (Dabaara), Rava Idli, Masala Dosa
mapsQuery: MTR Mavalli Tiffin Room Lalbagh Road Bangalore

name: Indian Coffee House
vibe: One of the most atmospheric cafes in the country. Run by a workers' cooperative since 1957, it serves strong filter coffee in tin cups with a side of old Bengaluru nostalgia.
mustTry: Filter Coffee, Toast and Egg, Masala Dosa
mapsQuery: Indian Coffee House Church Street Bangalore

name: Sendhoor Coffee
address: 153, 9th Cross, 2nd Main Road, Stage 1, Indiranagar, Bengaluru 560038
vibe: A no-nonsense traditional filter coffee counter in Indiranagar that the neighbourhood regulars swear by. The kaapi is strong and the price is fair — a hidden local gem.
mustTry: Filter Coffee, Samosa, Mirchi Bhajji

name: Kapi Katha
vibe: A charming little cafe in JP Nagar with a warm neighbourhood feel. The outdoor seating in the mornings is hard to beat — great for a slow Saturday with a good book.
mustTry: Filter Coffee, Latte, Banana Walnut Cake

name: Bloom & Bean
address: 19th Main Road, Sector 4, HSR Layout, Bengaluru 560102
vibe: An airy, plant-filled cafe in HSR that does both coffee and matcha beautifully. Dog-friendly patio and a calm weekend energy that keeps the regulars coming back.
mustTry: Matcha Latte, Iced Latte, Avocado Toast
mapsQuery: Bloom and Bean HSR Layout Bangalore

name: Suay
address: 7, 5th Phase, Outer Ring Road, JP Nagar, Bengaluru 560078
vibe: Bengaluru's dedicated Vietnamese coffee cafe. Egg coffee, condensed milk drip, phin filters — Suay brings Hanoi to South BLR with authenticity and warmth.
mustTry: Cà Phê Trứng (Egg Coffee), Cà Phê Sữa Đá (Iced Milk Coffee), Bánh Mì
mapsQuery: Suay Vietnamese Coffee JP Nagar Bangalore

name: By 2 Coffee
vibe: Named after the classic Bengaluru tradition of splitting a cup of kaapi between two glasses. No-frills, authentic filter coffee the way the city grew up with it.
mustTry: By 2 Filter Coffee, Vada, Samosa

name: GB Roasters
address: 12th Main Road, HAL 2nd Stage, Indiranagar, Bengaluru 560038
vibe: A micro-roastery that roasts small, seasonal lots and takes every cup seriously. The rotating single-origin menu is genuinely exciting — a must-visit for coffee enthusiasts.
mustTry: Rotating Single Origin, White Honey Process, Natural Ethiopia

name: Grumpy Girl Coffee
vibe: Creative, seasonal, and unafraid to experiment. Grumpy Girl makes drinks that photograph beautifully and taste even better — the kind of cafe that makes you follow them on Instagram.
mustTry: Seasonal Special, Brown Butter Latte, Signature Cold Brew

name: The Filter Coffee
vibe: South Indian filter coffee done right in a laid-back Domlur setting. Great for a late-morning idli-kaapi combo before the rush.
mustTry: Filter Coffee, Masala Dosa, Pongal

name: Cafe Azzure
vibe: A rooftop spot on MG Road with city views and a relaxed atmosphere. Popular for evening dates and casual meetups — the combination of a good latte and the breeze is hard to beat.
mustTry: Cappuccino, Chocolate Fondue, Tiramisu

name: Kalmane Koffee
vibe: Rooted in Coorg coffee estates, Kalmane keeps the tradition of pure estate-to-cup filter coffee. A Malleshwaram staple with honest pricing and consistently great decoction.
mustTry: Estate Filter Coffee, Chicory Coffee, Bun Butter

name: Kohi Coffee
vibe: Inspired by Japanese coffee culture — slow, quiet, and precise. Kohi makes brilliant pour-overs and filter coffees in a space that respects the ritual of the cup.
mustTry: Japanese Pour Over, Iced Coffee, Matcha Latte

name: Stir Coffee
vibe: A no-drama specialty cafe in Koramangala that regulars rely on. Consistent espresso, reliable Wi-Fi, and a comfortable setup for long work sessions.
mustTry: Espresso, Latte, Cold Brew, Banana Bread

name: Koshy's
vibe: Bengaluru's most beloved old-school diner, open since 1952. Writers, journalists, professors, and politicians have all found their corner here. The filter coffee is timeless and the ambience is irreplaceable.
mustTry: Filter Coffee, Pork Chops, Caramel Custard

name: Brahmin's Coffee Bar
vibe: The breakfast institution of Bengaluru. Get here before 8 AM or queue up — the vada and idli with that frothy filter coffee is a morning ritual for half the city.
mustTry: Filter Coffee (Dabaara), Vada, Idli Sambar

name: Veena Stores
vibe: Open since 1936, Veena Stores is where Malleshwaram mornings begin. Piping hot filter coffee in a dabaara set, served through a tiny window to a queue of regulars who've been coming for decades.
mustTry: Filter Kaapi, Masala Dosa, Vada

name: Vidyarthi Bhavan
address: 32, Gandhi Bazaar Main Road, Basavanagudi, Bengaluru 560004
vibe: An icon since 1943 — the masala dosa and filter coffee pairing here is considered the gold standard in Bengaluru. The vintage interior and old-world service haven't changed in decades. Queues are guaranteed.
mustTry: Masala Dosa, Filter Coffee, Kesari Bath

name: Central Tiffin Room (CTR)
vibe: Famous for the original benne (butter) masala dosa. CTR's filter coffee is the real Bengaluru deal — thick decoction, frothy milk, served in a traditional tumbler. This is the breakfast Bengaluru runs on.
mustTry: Benne Masala Dosa, Filter Coffee, Kesari Bath
mapsQuery: Central Tiffin Room CTR Malleshwaram Bangalore

name: Third Culture Bakery
vibe: Blending South Indian flavours with American pastry techniques — think miso kouign-amann and cardamom croissants. The coffee is excellent and the bakes sell out fast. Get here early.
mustTry: Cardamom Croissant, Miso Kouign-Amann, Pour Over

name: Smoor Chocolates
vibe: Belgian chocolate meets specialty espresso. Smoor is indulgence done right — the hot chocolate is almost unfairly good, and the bonbons are perfect to take home. A special-occasion stop.
mustTry: Single Origin Hot Chocolate, Bonbons, Affogato

name: Church Street Social
vibe: Laptop work, board games, craft cocktails, and a buzzing crowd from noon onwards. Social's co-work-cafe hybrid format was ahead of its time — still one of the best places to spend a productive afternoon in central BLR.
mustTry: Cold Coffee, Social Platter, Cocktail Cold Brew

name: Glenn's Bakehouse
address: 2913, 12th A Main Road, HAL 2nd Stage, Indiranagar, Bengaluru 560038
vibe: A warm neighbourhood bakery with proper espresso. The sourdough and croissants are baked fresh every morning and the small cafe space fills up fast with laptop workers and friends catching up.
mustTry: Sourdough, Almond Croissant, Flat White

name: Black Baza Coffee
vibe: Coffee with a conscience — Black Baza works directly with smallholder farmers across India's forest regions and pays above-market rates. The coffee quality reflects that care at every step.
mustTry: Anaerobic Natural Pour Over, Wild Forest Blend, Cold Brew

name: Toit Brewpub
vibe: One of India's best brewpubs, Toit is an Indiranagar landmark. The house-brewed ales and stouts pair well with the excellent food menu. Great patio seating for Bengaluru's famous evenings.
mustTry: Toit Weiss, Windmills Wit, Wood-fired Pizza

name: Lagom
vibe: Inspired by the Swedish concept of just the right amount — Lagom's menu is focused, the space is clean, and the coffee is very good. A thoughtful Koramangala alternative to the busier chains.
mustTry: Single Origin Filter, Oat Flat White, Cardamom Bun

name: The Permit Room
vibe: A concept bar-cafe that celebrates the South Indian filter coffee tradition through a cocktail lens. The signature 'Kaapi Cocktails' are a real conversation starter. Fun, noisy, and worth visiting.
mustTry: Filter Coffee Old Fashioned, Rasam Bloody Mary, Pandi Curry
mapsQuery: The Permit Room Church Street Bangalore

name: Drip & Grind
vibe: Whitefield's go-to specialty spot for the tech crowd. Close to ITPL, it's packed with laptop workers from 9 AM — the espresso is sharp and the Wi-Fi holds up under load.
mustTry: Espresso, Cold Brew, Banana Bread

name: The Slow Press
vibe: Everything here is intentionally slow — pour-overs take time, conversations happen, phones get put down. One of Indiranagar's best-kept specialty secrets for those who actually want to taste the coffee.
mustTry: Pour Over, Aeropress, Batch Brew

name: Onesta
vibe: Bengaluru's beloved thin-crust pizza chain that started as a neighbourhood spot. Great for relaxed group dinners — the coffee is solid and the wood-fired pizzas are genuinely excellent.
mustTry: Wood-fired Pizza, Cold Coffee, Tiramisu

name: The Humming Tree
vibe: Bengaluru's premier indie music venue doubles as a great late-evening cafe and bar. The outdoor courtyard fills up on gig nights — check their schedule before you go. Unforgettable when a good act is playing.
mustTry: Craft Beer, Cold Coffee, Check the Live Set

name: Third Wave Coffee
vibe: The East BLR outpost inside Phoenix Marketcity. Convenient for the Whitefield/Mahadevapura crowd — solid coffee, consistent quality, and a reliable workspace when you need it.
mustTry: Cold Brew, Oat Latte, Banana Bread
mapsQuery: Third Wave Coffee Whitefield Phoenix Bangalore

name: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters
vibe: West Bengaluru's Blue Tokai — same excellent roastery-grade coffee in a calm neighbourhood setting. Regulars from Rajajinagar, Vijayanagar, and Magadi Road swear by this outpost.
mustTry: Pour Over, Cold Brew, Espresso Tonic
mapsQuery: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters Rajajinagar Bangalore

name: Kapi Thevu
vibe: A small and earnest cafe that bridges the gap between specialty and filter — you can get a well-extracted espresso or a traditional South Indian kaapi here, and both are made with equal care.
mustTry: South Indian Filter Coffee, Espresso, Cold Brew

name: Thulp
vibe: Bengaluru's beloved burger joint with cold coffee good enough to stand on its own. The shakes and cold brews are a perfect pairing with their stacked burgers — a go-to for a casual afternoon.
mustTry: Thulp Burger, Cold Coffee Shake, Peanut Butter Shake

name: SLV Coffee Bar
vibe: A true udupi-style filter coffee bar that Malleshwaram residents have been waking up with for decades. No chairs, no Wi-Fi, no frills — just the best standalone filter coffee in the neighbourhood.
mustTry: Filter Kaapi, Bonda, Vada

name: Woke
vibe: A conscious cafe with an entirely plant-based menu and single-origin coffee. The smoothie bowls and oat-milk lattes are excellent and the interior is designed to be photographed.
mustTry: Oat Matcha Latte, Smoothie Bowl, Black Espresso

name: Pour Over Cafe
vibe: HSR's dedicated pour-over cafe — small, focused, and very good at one thing. The barista explains each brew method if you're curious. Great for solo work or a quiet catch-up.
mustTry: V60 Pour Over, Chemex, Iced Filter Coffee
mapsQuery: Pour Over Cafe HSR Layout Bangalore

name: Cafe Plume
vibe: A lovely hidden cafe in Benson Town that the neighbourhood treats as its own. The pastries are homemade, the coffee is specialty-grade, and the vibe on a quiet weekday morning is unbeatable.
mustTry: Latte, Homemade Tart, Batch Brew

name: Window Seat Coffee
vibe: Named for its best seats — large windows overlooking a quiet Koramangala street. Great light, great coffee, and a comfortable vibe that makes you want to stay for one more cup.
mustTry: Iced Latte, Pour Over, Carrot Cake

name: Double Road Filter Coffee
vibe: A dependable traditional filter coffee counter on Jayanagar's Double Road. The decoction is made to order and the coffee hits hard — regulars stop here twice a day without thinking about it.
mustTry: Filter Coffee, Idli, Sambar Vada
mapsQuery: Filter Coffee Double Road Jayanagar Bangalore
